1.name: Now I Go
1.hand: Three signs in sequence: both bent hands drop downward sharply (NOW), dominant index finger points to own chest (I), both index fingers arc outward away from the body (GO). Each handshape must be distinct and clearly formed before transitioning.
1.face: Neutral, declarative expression sustained throughout the phrase. Eyebrows relaxed — this is a statement, not a question. No WH-question or yes/no brow movement required.
1.body: Upright torso across all three signs. Hands transition smoothly from the downward drop to chest point to forward arc. Natural chest-height positioning throughout.

2.name: Free Time I Want
2.hand: Three signs in sequence: dominant F-handshape or flat hands signing FREE-TIME (hands cross at wrists then open outward), dominant index finger points to own chest (I), both curved hands with palms up pull inward toward the body (WANT). Each sign must transition cleanly.
2.face: Relaxed, slightly desirous or wistful expression. Eyebrows may raise slightly during WANT to convey desire. Overall pleasant, wishful tone throughout the phrase.
2.body: Upright posture. The crossing and opening motion of FREE-TIME is at chest level. I returns to chest. WANT pulls both hands inward symmetrically at chest height.

3.name: Now We Play
3.hand: Three signs in sequence: both bent hands drop sharply (NOW), dominant index finger arcs from one side to the other indicating WE or both people (WE), both Y-hands rotate and wiggle at chest level (PLAY). Transitions between each sign should be fluid.
3.face: Light, enthusiastic, or pleasant expression throughout. Slight smile acceptable and natural. Eyebrows relaxed. Expression should convey a sense of fun or shared activity.
3.body: Upright, open, relaxed posture. WE gesture sweeps across at chest height. PLAY uses both wrists rotating freely. Shoulders relaxed and open throughout.

4.name: Later I Will Visit Friend
4.hand: Five signs in sequence: dominant L-hand rotates forward from vertical to horizontal (LATER), index finger to chest (I), both V-hands rotate in alternating circles (VISIT), both index fingers hooked together alternating (FRIEND), dominant index finger or hand projects forward to indicate WILL/future. Each handshape must be clearly formed.
4.face: Neutral, forward-looking expression. Eyebrows relaxed — this is a declarative future statement, not a question. Slight warmth acceptable for FRIEND.
4.body: Upright torso throughout all five signs. Hands transition at natural chest height. VISIT requires both hands rotating at midline. FRIEND requires both hands at chest level with alternating hook motion.

5.name: Nice To Meet You Again
5.hand: Four signs in sequence: dominant flat hand slides across the non-dominant open palm (NICE/CLEAN), both index fingers come together meeting at fingertips (MEET), dominant index finger points forward toward addressee (YOU), dominant bent hand or X-hand makes a repeated forward motion (AGAIN/REPEAT). Each sign must be distinct.
5.face: Warm, genuinely pleased expression throughout. Slight smile natural and appropriate. Eyebrows relaxed and friendly. Expression should convey sincere pleasure at the meeting.
5.body: Upright, open posture. Slight forward lean acceptable to convey warmth. MEET requires both hands coming together at mid-chest. YOU points outward clearly. Shoulders open and relaxed.

6.name: Now I Listen
6.hand: Three signs in sequence: both bent hands drop sharply downward (NOW), dominant index finger points to own chest (I), dominant cupped or L-hand moves to the ear area (LISTEN). The transition to the ear should be a clear, deliberate movement.
6.face: Attentive, focused expression. Head may tilt slightly toward the signing hand during LISTEN. Eyebrows may rise slightly to convey attentiveness. Overall engaged expression.
6.body: Upright torso. Slight head tilt toward the ear during LISTEN is natural. Dominant arm raises so the hand reaches ear level for LISTEN. Shoulders relaxed throughout.

7.name: Today They Swim Finish
7.hand: Four signs in sequence: both Y-hands or bent hands drop twice (TODAY), dominant hand points outward to indicate a third party THEY, both flat hands make forward breaststroke arcs (SWIM), both open hands flip outward sharply with palms facing away (FINISH). All four handshapes must be distinct and clear.
7.face: Neutral, declarative expression throughout. Eyebrows relaxed — this is a statement about past completed action. FINISH may carry a slightly emphatic expression to convey completion.
7.body: Upright torso. TODAY drops at waist level twice. SWIM involves forward sweeping arm motion. FINISH requires a sharp bilateral outward wrist flip clearly visible. Hands transition smoothly between all four signs.

8.name: Later They Will Go To Movies
8.hand: Five signs in sequence: dominant L-hand rotates forward from vertical to horizontal (LATER), dominant hand points outward indicating a third party (THEY), both index fingers arc outward (GO), dominant hand forms an M-shape or flat hand at the side of the face mimicking a movie screen (MOVIE), dominant index or hand projects forward indicating future (WILL). Each sign must be clearly formed.
8.face: Neutral, forward-looking, declarative expression. Eyebrows relaxed throughout — this is a future statement, not a question. Natural, informational tone.
8.body: Upright torso throughout all five signs. Hands transition smoothly at natural chest and face height. MOVIE may involve hand near the face. LATER and WILL both project forward in time.

9.name: Now I Draw
9.hand: Three signs in sequence: both bent hands drop sharply (NOW), dominant index finger points to own chest (I), dominant I-handshape traces a wavy line across the non-dominant open palm (DRAW). The drawing motion should be a clear wavy or zigzag trace across the flat non-dominant hand.
9.face: Neutral, focused expression. Eyebrows relaxed. No grammatical brow movement required — this is a declarative statement. Eye contact maintained.
9.body: Upright torso. NOW drops at chest level. I points to chest. DRAW requires both hands at chest height — non-dominant flat as surface, dominant tracing across it with a wavy motion.

10.name: Now I Go To Cafe
10.hand: Four signs in sequence: both bent hands drop sharply (NOW), dominant index finger points to own chest (I), both index fingers arc outward (GO), dominant C-handshape or fingerspelled C-A-F-E or a recognized sign for cafe/coffee near the mouth (CAFE). Each sign must be clearly formed with clean transitions.
10.face: Neutral to slightly purposeful or casual expression. Eyebrows relaxed — this is a casual declarative statement. Slight naturalness in expression appropriate for casual conversation.
10.body: Upright, relaxed posture throughout. Hands transition at chest height. GO arcs outward clearly. CAFE sign is near the mouth or chin area. Overall body language is casual and purposeful.
